name: Hiroo Onoda
biography: An Imperial Japanese Army intelligence officer who continued fighting in the Philippine jungle until 1974 because he did not receive orders to surrender. He was discovered by a Japanese explorer and only laid down his arms after being ordered to do so by his former commanding officer. He had waged a guerrilla campaign for nearly 30 years.

name: Isoroku Yamamoto
biography: Commander-in-Chief of the Combined Fleet, Yamamoto planned and executed the attack on Pearl Harbor and the Battle of Midway. Despite initially opposing war with the United States, he led the Imperial Japanese Navy's strategic offensives. He was killed when his transport aircraft was ambushed by US P-38 fighters following American codebreaking.

name: Ettore Muti
biography: Decorated Fascist aviator and Secretary of the National Fascist Party. He participated in the Second Italo-Ethiopian War and the Spanish Civil War. Among the most decorated Italian officers, he was shot by Italian authorities following the fall of Mussolini.

name: Rodolfo Graziani
biography: The last colonial governor of Italian Libya and commander of Italian forces in North Africa in 1940. Known as 'The Butcher of Fezzan' for his brutal suppression of Libyan rebels, he led the Western Desert Campaign until defeated by British forces under Wavell. He later commanded the Italian National Republican Army under German occupation.

name: Leon Degrelle
biography: Leader of the Belgian Rexist movement who volunteered to fight for Germany on the Eastern Front. He rose from private to SS-Sturmbannführer and commanded the 28th SS Volunteer Grenadier Division Wallonien. Hitler reputedly said he was 'the son he never had'. He escaped justice after the war by fleeing to Spain.
